etymology.description: 木(木) (mù, 'tree') with a dot to highlight the center -> emphasis on core -> vermilion
etymology.interpretation: 朱(朱) (zhū, 'vermilion') emphasizes the core or heartwood of a tree, symbolizing the earthy reddish color. The highlighted center of a tree reflects the vividness and importance of the vermilion dye.

examples.chinese: 她穿了一件艳丽的朱色旗袍(她穿了一件艷麗的朱色旗袍)。
examples.pinyin: Tā chuānle yī jiàn yànlì de zhū sè qípáo.
examples.english: She wore a striking vermilion-colored cheongsam.

examples.chinese: 这种画风以朱红为主色调(這種畫風以朱紅為主色調)。
examples.pinyin: Zhè zhǒng huà fēng yǐ zhū hóng wéi zhǔ sè diào.
examples.english: This painting style uses vermilion as the main color tone.

examples.chinese: 他的印章用朱色印泥(他的印章用朱色印泥)，显得格外显眼(顯得格外顯眼)。
examples.pinyin: Tā de yìnzhāng yòng zhū sè yìnní, xiǎnde géwài xiǎnyǎn.
examples.english: His seal used vermilion inkpad, which made it particularly eye-catching.
